meta.title: Visually hidden
meta.description: Makes content accessible to screen readers but hides it visually.

mdx:
## Basic usage Just wrap the content you want to hide visually, but keep accessible to screen readers, with the `VisuallyHidden` component. ```jsx const App = () => I am VisuallyHidden!; ``` ## Focusable content If the content you want to hide visually contains focusable elements, add the `isFocusable` property to the `VisuallyHidden` component. The content then becomes visible if the first focusable element inside is focused with the _keyboard_. ```jsx live const App = () => ( ); ``` ## Inline content `VisuallyHidden` uses a `
` element by default to render into the DOM. If you need to hide inline content, you can use the `as` prop to render a `` element instead, keeping the `html` semantics intact. ```jsx live const App = () => (

Text that will be visible. I will blend just in. Text that will be visible.

); ```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Button>
    <Icons.Delete />
    <VisuallyHidden>Delete item</VisuallyHidden>
  </Button>
);
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Provide text alternatives for icons:** Use VisuallyHidden to add descriptive
  text for icon-only buttons or links that is accessible to screen readers but
  visually hidden.
- **Don't hide important content:** Only use VisuallyHidden for supplementary
  text that provides context for assistive technologies, not for essential
  information that all users need.
- **Skip links:** Use VisuallyHidden to create skip navigation links that allow
  keyboard users to bypass repetitive content.
- **Form labels:** Use for additional context in form labels when visual design
  already makes the purpose clear.
- **Accessible names:** Provide accessible names for interactive elements that
  rely on visual cues (icons, colors, shapes).
- **Maintain focus visibility:** When VisuallyHidden contains focusable elements
  (like skip links), ensure they become visible when focused.
- **Screen reader only instructions:** Use for providing specific instructions
  that screen reader users need but sighted users don't.

### Resources

- [WebAIM - CSS in Action: Invisible Content](https://webaim.org/techniques/css/invisiblecontent/)
- [A11y Project - How to hide content](https://www.a11yproject.com/posts/how-to-hide-content/)
- [WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/bypass-blocks.html)
